architecture_overview: SpecForge AI follows a three-tier architecture: a Next.js 14 frontend, a FastAPI backend, and a PostgreSQL database. The backend exposes a REST API consumed by the frontend. All LLM calls are server-side through a provider-agnostic LLM abstraction. A reusable Skill Engine encapsulates each AI capability as a directory of instruction, template, and schema files.

components.name: Frontend (Next.js 14)
components.description: App Router with server components for SSR and client components for interactivity. Uses SWR for data fetching, tiptap for rich text editing, and Tailwind CSS for styling. Three-panel workspace layout at /projects/[id].

components.name: FastAPI Backend
components.description: Async REST API with Pydantic validation, CORS whitelist, security headers, and slowapi rate limiting. Mounts routers for projects, documents, specs, gaps, and reviews. Global exception handler produces consistent envelope responses.

components.name: Skill Engine
components.description: Loads skill directories (instruction.md + template.md + schema.json), renders Jinja2 prompts, calls the LLM provider, validates output against JSON Schema, and retries once on validation failure.

components.name: LLM Abstraction Layer
components.description: LLMProvider ABC with GeminiProvider (google-generativeai SDK) and MockProvider (fixture JSON files). Provider selected via LLM_PROVIDER env var; falls back to mock if GEMINI_API_KEY is absent.

components.name: Document Parser
components.description: Abstracts PDF (PyMuPDF), DOCX (python-docx), and TXT parsing. Storage abstraction keeps file paths swappable to Azure Blob later.

data_models.name: Project
data_models.description: UUID PK, name, description, soft-delete via deleted_at. Parent of all other entities.

data_models.name: Document
data_models.description: UUID PK, project FK (CASCADE), filename, mime_type, size_bytes, storage_path, extracted_text, parse_status (PG enum), parse_error.

data_models.name: ExtractedRequirement
data_models.description: UUID PK, project FK, document FK (SET NULL), category (PG enum), ext_id, text, source_reference, confidence (PG enum).

data_models.name: SpecVersion
data_models.description: UUID PK, project FK, spec_type (PG enum), version_number (per-project monotonic, allocated via SELECT FOR UPDATE), content_json (JSONB), content_markdown, is_edited.

data_models.name: GapQuestion
data_models.description: UUID PK, project FK, ext_id, question, category (PG enum), severity (PG enum), resolved, resolution_text (free-text answer fed into spec regen), resolved_at.

data_models.name: ReviewComment
data_models.description: UUID PK, project FK, spec_version FK (SET NULL), section, comment, severity (PG enum), category (PG enum), dismissed, dismissed_at.

tech_stack.frontend: Next.js 14, React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, tiptap, SWR
tech_stack.backend: FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, asyncpg, Alembic, Pydantic v2, slowapi
tech_stack.llm: Google Gemini 1.5 Flash via google-generativeai SDK

risks.mitigation: JSON Schema validation with one auto-retry and error-correction prompt

risks.risk: Large document text overflowing prompt context
risks.mitigation: Truncate document_text to 60,000 chars; log truncation warning
slowapi rate limit 30 req/min/IP on all LLM-triggering endpoints

risks.risk: Deferred auth leaves all endpoints open
risks.mitigation: CORS whitelist + rate limiting + security headers provide baseline protection in dev; auth planned for v2
